mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate increases with p-value below 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 consistently in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of triggered instances, or monthly unemployment spikes fail to predict short-term drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and residential demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in consumption and housing starts yield positive 60-day equity returns in greater than 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% year-over-year during a post-shock recovery phase confirms supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 2% fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, with mean forward returns below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% signals severe demand destruction that forces immediate equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP contractions below -0.5% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of observations, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive real policy conditions that suppress corporate margins and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation alongside stable or rising rates yields positive 60-day equity returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 during a late-cycle expansion phase confirms economic momentum that supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 200,000 fails to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, with mean forward returns negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals acute supply-side disruption that triggers immediate market repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops below -1.0% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in greater than 65% of instances, or the threshold signal shows no edge in walk-forward validation.
